# Spare Parts Dealer (Ladipo / Nnewi) Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-spare-parts-spare-parts-dealer
**Vertical slug:** spare-parts
**VN-ID:** VN-COM-001
**Niche family:** NF-COM-AUT (variant of used-car-dealer)
**Family role:** variant
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's auto spare parts market is one of West Africa's largest, anchored by Ladipo International Motor Parts Market (Lagos, estimated 100,000+ traders) and Nnewi Auto Parts City (Anambra, Nigeria's spare parts manufacturing hub). Parts span Japanese (Toyota, Honda), Korean (Hyundai, Kia), and Chinese brands. Brand authenticity (genuine vs imitation/Taiwan) is the #1 customer concern. WhatsApp parts enquiry and price check is standard practice. Mechanics, fleet operators, and transport companies are bulk buyers.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Mechanic Adetokunbo, 32, Lagos**: Needs a radiator fan for a customer's 2014 Toyota Camry. WhatsApps to check stock and price before sending his apprentice. Wants brand-new or tested tokunbo.
- **Fleet manager Baba, 45, Kano**: Sources parts for 20-truck haulage fleet. Needs supplier with bulk pricing, fast turnaround, and delivery to Kano. Calls or WhatsApps.
- **Self-builder Mrs Eze, 50, Onitsha**: Replacing engine mount on personal vehicle. Visits physically but found dealer via WhatsApp group first.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (shop name, specialty — "Ladipo Trusted"), WhatsApp parts enquiry CTA, featured parts grid (6 items with prices), trust strip (SON, CAC, Ladipo provenance), about blurb
- **About**: Shop history, sourcing network (Ladipo, Nnewi, direct importers), staff expertise, CAC reg, SON compliance
- **Services (Parts Catalogue)**: Parts list with ₦ pricing, brand/model filter suggestion, WhatsApp for hard-to-find parts
- **Contact**: WhatsApp parts enquiry (primary), location (Ladipo market address or shop), enquiry form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Expert, parts-knowledgeable, authenticity-focused. "Genuine Parts Only", "Ladipo Trusted", "Workshop Pricing Available" language. Reference Ladipo, Nnewi, Aspamda. Primary CTA: "Find a Part via WhatsApp". Secondary: "Browse Parts Catalogue".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) conformity certificate
- CAC registration
- Ladipo Market / Nnewi provenance statement
- Customs import documentation for genuine parts
- NAFDAC clearance for lubricants (if stocked)
- Anti-counterfeit warranty statement
- Mechanic/workshop bulk pricing availability

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Spare parts shelves — organised, labelled, branded items (Toyota, Honda logos visible)
2. Ladipo market context — busy, traders with parts
3. Mechanic installing genuine part — workshop, undercar lighting
4. SON certificate on wall — authenticity trust signal
5. Parts delivery — packaged box being delivered to customer/workshop
6. Close-up of genuine part — hologram seal, brand marking
7. Shop exterior — professional signage, Ladipo market address

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] SON conformity certificate for imported parts
- [ ] NAFDAC clearance for lubricants/chemicals
- [ ] CAC business registration
- [ ] Customs import duty compliance documentation
- [ ] Anti-counterfeit compliance statement
- [ ] NDPR consent on enquiry form

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp parts enquiry CTA prominent
- [x] SON/CAC trust signals
- [x] Ladipo/Nnewi provenance reference
- [x] ₦ pricing per part
- [x] Nigerian vehicle brands referenced (Toyota, Honda, Kia)
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on enquiry form
